questions.question: Explain the three main components of Branch and Bound algorithm with brief description of each.
keyPoints: Branching: Dividing problem into subproblems creating child nodes
Bounding: Computing bound (lower/upper) for each node to estimate best achievable solution
Pruning: Eliminating nodes whose bounds indicate no better solution possible

questions.question: Differentiate between FIFO and LIFO search strategies in Branch and Bound with respect to their exploration order and use cases.
keyPoints: FIFO uses queue, explores level by level (Breadth-First), systematic but may explore many nodes before finding good solution
LIFO uses stack, explores depth first (Depth-First), may find good solution quickly but less systematic
FIFO suitable for minimization, LIFO may be better when good heuristic solution needed quickly

questions.question: Define the following terms in Branch and Bound: (i) Live Node (ii) E-node (iii) Promising Node
keyPoints: Live Node: Generated but not yet expanded node, whose children are not yet created
E-node: Node currently being expanded, children are being generated
Promising Node: Node whose bound indicates potential for better solution than current best

questions.question: How is the bound function used for pruning in minimization problems?
keyPoints: For minimization, compute lower bound for each node
If lower bound >= current best solution found, prune the node
This is because no solution in that subspace can be better than current best

questions.question: Mention at least three combinatorial optimization problems that can be solved using Branch and Bound technique.
keyPoints: Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP)
0/1 Knapsack Problem
Job Assignment Problem
Graph Coloring
Flow Shop Scheduling

questions.question: Compare Branch and Bound with Backtracking approach for solving combinatorial problems.
keyPoints: Backtracking explores all possibilities until solution found; B&B uses bounds to prune
B&B typically more efficient as it eliminates large search spaces
Backtracking guaranteed to find solution if exists; B&B may prune incorrectly if bounds wrong
B&B maintains best solution found; backtracking finds one solution

questions.question: Explain the concept of state space tree in Branch and Bound with its relevance.
keyPoints: State space tree represents all possible solutions as nodes
Root represents empty/initial state
Each level represents one decision/variable assignment
Leaf nodes represent complete solutions
B&B traverses this tree with pruning based on bounds

questions.question: Solve the following 0/1 Knapsack problem using Branch and Bound: Capacity W=10, Items: (w1=2,v1=10), (w2=4,v2=10), (w3=6,v3=12), (w4=9,v4=18). Show the state space tree exploration with pruning decisions.
keyPoints: Calculate bound using fractional knapsack for upper bound
Initialize upper bound using greedy solution
Explore nodes level by level with FIFO
Prune nodes where bound <= current best
Track maximum value achievable within capacity
Show step-by-step tree with promising/non-promising nodes

questions.question: Apply Branch and Bound to solve the following Traveling Salesman Problem: 4 cities with distance matrix. Find minimum tour cost. Also explain how pruning reduces search space.
keyPoints: Start with initial upper bound from nearest neighbor heuristic
Calculate lower bound for partial tours using reduced cost matrix
Branch on next city selection
Prune when partial tour cost >= current best
Show complete exploration or optimal solution found
Explain how many branches pruned vs exhaustive 3!=6 possibilities

questions.question: Explain the Branch and Bound algorithm for the Job Assignment Problem. Illustrate with an example of 3 workers and 3 jobs.
keyPoints: Problem: Assign N jobs to N workers minimizing total cost
State space: N! permutations explored with pruning
Lower bound computation: Sum of minimum costs in each row/column not yet assigned
Prune when partial cost >= current best assignment
Explain with 3x3 cost matrix example
Show optimal assignment and pruning decisions

questions.question: Design a Branch and Bound algorithm for finding minimum spanning tree in a weighted graph. Explain all components including branching, bounding, and pruning strategy.
keyPoints: Root node represents partial tree or empty set
Branching adds one edge to existing spanning tree
Bounding: Use minimum spanning tree of remaining graph as lower bound
Prune if bound >= current best MST weight
Alternatively use cut property for lower bound
Explain with example graph
